title: Quiz: Photocatalysis
description: Test your understanding of photocatalytic processes

questions.question: For overall water splitting, what are the requirements for band edge positions?

options.text: CB > 0 V and VB < 1.23 V vs NHE
CB < 0 V and VB > 1.23 V vs NHE
Both CB and VB above 0 V vs NHE
Band gap must equal exactly 1.23 eV

questions.explanation: For water splitting, the conduction band must be more negative than the H⁺/H₂ potential (0 V vs NHE) for hydrogen evolution, and the valence band must be more positive than the O₂/H₂O potential (1.23 V vs NHE) for oxygen evolution.

questions.question: Why does standard DFT-GGA significantly underestimate band gaps?

options.text: It uses too small basis sets
It has self-interaction errors and lacks derivative discontinuity
It cannot handle periodic systems
It ignores spin-orbit coupling

questions.explanation: DFT-GGA underestimates band gaps due to self-interaction errors (electrons spuriously interacting with themselves) and the absence of the derivative discontinuity of the exact exchange-correlation functional. Hybrid functionals like HSE06 partially correct this.

questions.question: What is the main limitation of Fe₂O₃ (hematite) as a photocatalyst?

options.text: Its band gap is too large for visible light
It has very short hole diffusion length (~2 nm)
Its conduction band is too negative
It dissolves quickly in water

questions.explanation: Fe₂O₃ has good optical properties (Eg ~2.1 eV, visible light absorption) but extremely poor charge transport with hole diffusion lengths of only 2-4 nm. This means photogenerated carriers recombine before reaching the surface unless the material is nanostructured.

questions.question: What is the difference between direct and indirect band gap semiconductors for photocatalysis?

options.text: Direct gaps have stronger light absorption but shorter carrier lifetimes
Indirect gaps absorb more light
Direct gaps require smaller particle sizes
There is no practical difference

questions.explanation: Direct band gap materials have vertical transitions in k-space, giving strong absorption (α ∝ √(E-Eg)). Indirect gaps require phonon assistance, giving weaker absorption (α ∝ (E-Eg)²) but longer carrier lifetimes since radiative recombination is also suppressed.

questions.question: Adding a co-catalyst like Pt to TiO₂ improves photocatalytic H₂ evolution by providing better reaction sites, not by changing the light absorption.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: True. Co-catalysts like Pt provide low-barrier sites for the hydrogen evolution reaction and help with charge separation (electrons migrate to Pt islands). They don't significantly change the light absorption, which is determined by the semiconductor photocatalyst.

questions.question: Which strategies can narrow the band gap of oxide photocatalysts? (Select all)

options.text: N-doping (raising the valence band)
Oxygen vacancy creation (mid-gap states)
Forming solid solutions with smaller-gap materials
Increasing crystallite size

questions.explanation: N-doping introduces N 2p states above the O 2p valence band, raising it. Oxygen vacancies can create donor states below the conduction band. Solid solutions (like GaN-ZnO) provide continuous band gap tuning. Crystallite size affects quantum confinement but typically increases (not decreases) the effective gap.
